title: Generate recovery keypair and mnemonic on signup
description:
Generate a BIP-39 recovery phrase for each user at signup. This becomes their identity backup.

## Why

Server holds the signing key. If server dies, key dies, user cannot prove DID ownership. Recovery key solves this — user holds a second rotation key that can update did:plc even after server death.

## Implementation

1. On user signup, generate recovery keypair:
   - Generate 256-bit entropy
   - Encode as BIP-39 mnemonic (24 words)
   - Derive Ed25519 keypair from mnemonic seed

2. Include recovery pubkey in did:plc rotationKeys (alongside server key)

3. Display recovery phrase to user ONCE:
   - Modal/interstitial they cannot skip
   - Checkbox: "I have saved my recovery phrase"
   - DO NOT store the phrase or private key server-side

4. Store only: recovery_pubkey, recovery_did_key, phrase_shown_at

## Code sketch

```python
from mnemonic import Mnemonic
from nacl.signing import SigningKey

def generate_recovery_key():
    m = Mnemonic("english")
    phrase = m.generate(strength=256)
    seed = m.to_seed(phrase, passphrase="")
    privkey = SigningKey(seed[:32])
    pubkey = privkey.verify_key
    did_key = pubkey_to_did_key(pubkey.encode())
    return phrase, did_key  # phrase shown to user, did_key stored
```

## Dependencies

- python-mnemonic (or JS equivalent)
- Runs BEFORE did:plc registration (need both keys for rotationKeys)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Signup generates 24-word mnemonic
- [ ] User sees phrase before account is usable
- [ ] Phrase is NOT stored server-side
- [ ] Recovery pubkey stored in user record
- [ ] did:plc rotationKeys includes both server and recovery keys
close_reason: Recovery keypair + 24-word BIP-39 mnemonic generated on persona create, shown once via stderr
